flying_page.title: Flying Bird
flying_page.subtitle: A cross-platform mobile clone of the classic Flappy Bird, optimized for Android devices.

about.title: Mobile Development Journey
desc: Flying Bird is a personal project developed to explore the mobile pipeline within Unreal Engine.
As a solo developer, I handled the entire process: from C++ gameplay logic and procedural spawning to Android-specific configurations like touch inputs and icon management.
The goal was to create a polished, lightweight experience that maintains consistent performance across both mobile and desktop platforms.

gameplay.title: Hard-to-Master Gameplay
items: • Physics-Based Movement: Precise impulse-based logic for the bird's flight, tuned for responsive feedback on touchscreens.
• Infinite Spawning: A procedural system that generates and recycles obstacles (pipes) to create a seamless infinite loop.
• Adaptive Difficulty: Obstacle placement and gaps designed to provide a challenging but fair progression.
• Audio Feedback: Integrated sound system with real-time mute functionality and saved preferences.

tech.title: The Mobile Pipeline
items: • Android Integration: Managed the full deployment pipeline, including SDK/NDK setup, Android manifests, and custom icon sets.
• C++ Spawner Logic: Developed a dedicated PipesSpawner class to manage the lifecycle of obstacles efficiently.
• SaveGame System: Implementation of a local persistence layer for high scores and audio settings using C++.
• Input Scalability: Unified input mapping that handles both screen taps (Android) and mouse clicks/keyboard (Windows).

systems.title: UX & UI Polish
items: • Mobile HUD: A simplified UI featuring a pre-game countdown and a dynamic scoring system tailored for small screens.
• State Management: Clean transition logic between the main menu, gameplay loop, and the game-over screen.
• Performance Tuning: Optimized textures and materials to ensure high frame rates on a wide range of Android devices.

mobile.title: Android Integration
mobile.desc: Focus on the mobile-first development approach, ensuring correct UI scaling, touch responsiveness, and platform-specific assets.
mobile.img1: Platform Setup: Configuration of the Android manifest and project settings to handle device orientation and icon packages.
mobile.img2: Mobile UX: Implementation of an on-screen countdown and large, accessible buttons designed for thumb interaction.
mobile.img3: Cross-Platform Build: The game running on Android, demonstrating successful porting of the C++ core logic to the ARM architecture.

spawner.title: Procedural Spawner Logic
spawner.desc: A look at the C++ backbone responsible for the infinite gameplay loop and obstacle randomization.
spawner.img1: PipesSpawner Class: Analysis of the spawner logic which handles the timing, vertical randomization, and recycling of pipe actors.
spawner.img2: Object Pooling Concept: How the system manages the lifecycle of pipes to minimize memory allocation during gameplay.

savegame.title: Persistence & Data
savegame.desc: Management of local player data, focusing on high score tracking and user settings.
savegame.img1: High Score Logic: Implementation of the SaveGame class to store the best score locally between play sessions.
savegame.img2: Settings Persistence: System for saving and loading audio states (mute/unmute) so player preferences are remembered on restart.

cta.title: Ready to fly?
cta.subtitle: Download the APK for Android or the Windows version on itch.io.
